html:
Klas Horn is the name of several historical figures:

  • Klas Henriksson Horn (Died in the 1520s), a lawman and member of the Swedish Council of State.
  • Klas Kristersson Horn af Åminne (1517-1566), Baron of Åminne, an admiral.
  • Klas Karlsson Horn (1583-1632), Marshal of the Realm and Governor of Kanckas.
  • Klas Horn (1587–1651), Baron of Åminne, member of the Swedish Council of State, father of Krister Horn (1622-1692) and Bengt Horn (1623-1678).
  • Claes Horn (1755–1823), Count, Lieutenant General, one of the Lords of the Realm.
  • Clas Fredrik Horn af Åminne (1763-1823).
  • Claes Fredrik Horn af Åminne (1791–1865).

And also three ships:

  • Klas Horn, a Finnish cannonboat launched in 1892.
  • HMS Claes Horn, a cruiser launched in 1898.
  • HMS Klas Horn (3), a destroyer launched in 1931.

How certain are we about this?

From the Vasa era onwards, individuals are well-documented in archives. The uncertainty lies more in interpretation and how later generations perceived them – not least because those in power themselves played a role in shaping historical narratives.
tldr: The name "Klas Horn" has been used by several significant figures in Swedish history, including nobles and military personnel. It has also been given to three different warships.
lattlast:
There are many historical figures named Klas Horn or with similar names. These individuals often held prominent positions in society, such as members of the national council who participated in the country's highest decision-making bodies, or military leaders.

In addition to these people, the name has been used for three different ships: a cannon boat from the late 1800s, a cruiser, and a destroyer that were used by the navy during the 20th century.

Because these individuals lived long ago, there is extensive documentation about them in archives. However, it is more difficult to know exactly how they were perceived by other people at the time, as those who held power often influenced how their history was recorded.
